sections.paragraph:
ἀλυσιτελής, ές (Socrates 1, 8 p. 22, 21 Malherbe; Hippocr., Pla., X. et al.; PSI 441, 21; PCairZen 481, 2 [both III b.c.] PTebt 68, 31 [117/116 b.c.]; Philo; LStraub, Philol 70, 1911, 157–60; a commercial t.t. ‘not covering one’s expenses’; s. DELG s.v. λύω and τέλος) unprofitable ἀλυσιτελὲς ὑμῖν τοῦτο that would be of no help to you Hb 13:17; but ἀ. can also be used positively, harmful (Polyb. 11, 4, 7; 28, 6, 4 al.; Philo, Spec. Leg. 1, 100 ἡ οἴνου χρῆσις ἀ.).—M-M.

Malherbe Malherbe = AM., various works

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Philol Philol , Zeitschrift für das klassische Altertum—List 6

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 48
